site_goal: Position Xerox Pressing Co. as a fiercely independent riso-print record label, drive vinyl pre-orders, surface upcoming tour dates, and convert listeners into fans of specific roster artists through a zine-style browsing experience anchored by a persistent audio player bar.
description: A gritty, photocopied-zine feeling label site for Xerox Pressing Co., a fictional Brooklyn-and-Glasgow indie label specializing in noise rock, post-punk, and experimental electronics. The whole site reads like a stapled fanzine printed at 2am: newsprint cream paper texture, riso pink and electric cyan misregistered ink overlays, oversized black brutalist headlines that often run off the page edge, halftone photos of artists, hand-drawn arrows, paste-up tape marks at corners of cards, marker-scrawl annotations on release covers. A sticky audio player bar pinned to the bottom of every page plays a curated label sampler with track skip, scrubber, and a 'now spinning' marquee. The roster page is an asymmetric grid of artist cards built like trading-card cutouts; the catalog page renders each release as a vinyl-with-sleeve mockup the user can flip to see the B-side. Tour dates live on a typewriter-style list with a sold-out stamp motif. Merch is a contact-sheet grid. The history page is a long scrolling zine spread with column-broken text, footnotes, and inkjet-streak dividers.
motif: photocopied zine paste-up with misregistered riso ink, halftone artist portraits, marker scrawls, masking-tape corners, and oversized condensed grotesk headlines smashed against a newsprint-cream paper grain

pages.layout_pattern: zine cover splash with oversized misregistered headline, featured release jacket, three latest news pull-quote cards, and an embedded sampler tracklist that loads into the sticky player
sections: masthead with label name treated as a smudged cyan-over-pink riso block
lead headline of the month set in oversized condensed grotesk running off the right edge
featured new release card with vinyl mockup, artist credit, pre-order pill
three news/zine teaser cards with date stamps and marker underlines
label sampler tracklist (load-into-player) with track numbers in oversized digits
tear-off newsletter strip with dashed scissor cut and 'subscribe to the dispatch' field
sticky audio player bar pinned to bottom

pages.layout_pattern: asymmetric trading-card grid of artist portraits with tape corners, hand-drawn underlines, and chip-style genre filters across the top
sections: page header reading 'The Roster' with cyan strike-through
genre filter chips (noise, post-punk, ambient, dub, no-wave, electronic)
12-16 artist cards in irregular column widths with halftone portraits, hometown, latest release thumbnail, and a 'hear them' play button that loads their last single into the player
submissions / demo policy footer block typed on a typewriter

pages.layout_pattern: long-form zine spread for a single artist: big halftone hero, three-column biography with pull quotes, vertical discography rail, and embedded tour dates
sections: huge halftone hero portrait with the artist name overlaid in oversized stencil type
metadata strip: hometown, formed, members
three-column biography with footnote markers and pull-quote callouts
discography rail showing all releases as small vinyl mockups
selected press quotes set as torn-paper clippings
artist tour dates list filtered to this artist
social links and contact-for-booking line

pages.layout_pattern: catalog grid of vinyl-with-sleeve mockups, each flippable to reveal B-side; filterable by format and year with a left-rail catalog-number index
sections: left rail with catalog number index (XRX-001 through XRX-047) acting as anchor links
filter row: format (LP / 7-inch / cassette / digital), year, in-stock toggle
grid of release cards, each a vinyl-sticking-out-of-sleeve mockup with cyan/pink color variant swatches
click-to-flip reveals B-side tracklist and liner-note excerpt
pre-order banner for upcoming pressings styled as a stamped notice

pages.layout_pattern: single-release deep page with large sleeve art, tracklist with per-track preview, pressing variant selector, and credits ledger
sections: large front sleeve art with riso-style overlay
metadata column: artist, catalog number, release date, format, pressing size
tracklist with each row having a play button that cues the sticky player and an A/B side divider
pressing variant selector swatches (black, cyan splatter, pink marble) with stock state
buy block with format prices and add-to-cart pill
credits ledger: produced by, mastered by, sleeve art by, recorded at
liner notes set as multi-column zine copy

pages.layout_pattern: typewriter-style chronological tour-date list with monospace columns, sold-out rubber-stamp marks, and a region filter
sections: page header 'On The Road' with riso-misregistered subtitle
filter bar: artist, region (US East / US West / UK / EU), 'tickets left' toggle
tour date rows in monospace: date | city | venue | artist | support | tickets/sold-out stamp
embedded poster gallery thumbnails for the current touring cycle
promoter contact block at the bottom

pages.layout_pattern: contact-sheet grid of merch product photos with hand-numbered captions, plus a category sidebar and a sticky mini-cart pill
sections: category sidebar: shirts, hoodies, totes, posters, pins, bundles
product grid of square photos shot like a screen-printer's contact sheet with frame numbers underneath
product card hover reveals price and 'add to bag'
shipping policy bar in typewriter type
bundle promo banner (LP + tee + zine)

pages.layout_pattern: long scrolling zine spread chaptered by year, with multi-column text, photocopied photographs, pull quotes, footnotes, and an annotated catalog timeline at the bottom
sections: chapter 01 — 2014: a basement and a 4-track
chapter 02 — 2017: the first sold-out pressing
chapter 03 — 2020: pressing plants and pandemics
chapter 04 — 2023: the Glasgow annex
pull-quote callouts misregistered in pink
footnote column with asterisk references
annotated horizontal timeline of every catalog number, ending with the latest release
colophon: who runs this label, mailing address, demo policy

message_intent: we are loud, independent, and we press our own records
every artist on this roster has a voice worth hearing in full
buying a record here directly supports the band
come see these bands live in a small room near you
this label has a history and a point of view
content_model: Artist: name, hometown, genre tags, bio, photo, linked releases, tour dates, socials
Release: title, artist, catalog number, format (12-inch, 7-inch, cassette, digital), pressing variant, release date, tracklist, sleeve art front/back, audio previews, buy link, liner notes
Tour date: date, city, venue, support acts, ticket link, sold-out flag
Merch item: title, artist (or label), photo set, sizes, price, stock state
Zine entry: chapter title, year, body copy with footnotes, archival photos, pull quotes
Audio track: title, artist, release, duration, file
interactions: sticky bottom audio player bar with play/pause, prev/next, scrubber, volume, now-spinning marquee, and queue popout
click a release sleeve to flip it and read the B-side tracklist
filter roster by genre tag chips (noise, post-punk, ambient, dub, no-wave)
tour list filter by region and 'has tickets left' toggle
merch size selector and add-to-cart pill with cart counter in the header
newsletter signup styled as a tear-off bottom-of-page strip with dashed scissor line
asset_needs: halftone black-and-white artist portrait photos (12-16 artists)
front and back sleeve mockups for ~24 releases
vinyl label center artwork for each release
riso pink and cyan texture overlays (grain, ink splatter, misregistration)
hand-drawn arrow, star, and asterisk SVG marks
audio waveform graphics for player bar
mobile_behavior: Sticky audio player collapses to a slim bar with cover thumbnail, title marquee, and play toggle. Roster grid stacks into a single column of taped-down cards. Release flip becomes a tap-to-flip card. Tour dates remain a typewriter list. Header navigation collapses into a stamped 'menu' button revealing a full-screen zine-style nav sheet.
